critic_findings.message: The allow entry for server/managed-terminal.ts masks a stale coverage report, not a genuine untestability. The coverage-summary.json on disk was produced by a terminal-ws-scoped test run (only terminal-ws.ts shows non-zero coverage; managed-terminal.ts shows 0/82 lines covered), yet tests.json proves the gate selected managed-terminal-detector.test.ts and managed-terminal.test.ts — the report and the test selection contradict each other. The writer's justification ('import type pulls managed-terminal.ts into scope') is mechanically incorrect: TypeScript erases import type at compile time, so it cannot influence runtime coverage scope. The allow entry must be removed; the writer must re-run gate-tests.sh (which will select the correct managed-terminal*.test.ts files) and then gate-coverage.sh to produce a consistent report before committing rq.config.json.
